title: AI Operating System for Marketing Agencies
metaDescription: Install and run an AI Operating System for your marketing agency. We handle lead reply, onboarding, reporting, and follow-up so the team gets the hours back.
excerpt: An AI Operating System is the layer that runs the repeated work in the background for a marketing agency. Here is what we install, in what order, and what done looks like.

sections.intro:
The problem most marketing agency owners describe is the same one: every new client request rebuilds itself from scratch. New leads sit in the inbox until someone has 20 minutes. Status updates get written by hand. Reports get rebuilt every month. Most of that is not strategy. It is the same work, repeated, by people who should be doing harder things.

An AI Operating System (AIOS) is the layer that runs the repeated work in the background. For a marketing agency, that means every new lead is answered in under 60 seconds, every client gets the same crisp onboarding, monthly reports draft themselves from live data, and briefs land on Monday without anyone asking. The team gets back the hours they were spending on coordination.

This page is the overview. Below is what we install, in what order, and what done looks like for an agency your size.
sections.fourCFit:
An AIOS is built on four layers. We install them in this order:

  • Context: the system knows your agency. Your offers, the voice you use with clients, your standard processes, the decisions you have already made. Without this, every prompt has to re-explain who you are.
  • Connections: the system reaches your tools. Your inbox, your calendar, your CRM (HubSpot, Pipedrive, whatever you use), your project tool, your reporting source. It works from live data, never copy-paste.
  • Capabilities: done-for-you workflows. Reply to a new lead. Send a kickoff package. Draft a monthly client report. Triage the inbox. These are the things that show up in your day.
  • Cadence: it runs on a schedule, without being asked. A brief lands in your inbox at 7am. A weekly status goes to each client on Friday. A monthly report ships on the first.

Most agencies already have Context and Connections half built (it lives in their heads and in their tools). The AIOS makes it durable, then layers Capabilities on top, then sets the Cadence so the laptop can stay closed.
sections.whatDoneLooksLike:
Done for a marketing agency typically looks like this in the first 90 days:

  • Every new lead gets a personalized reply in under 60 seconds, in your voice, with your booking link. See 60-Second Lead Response for Marketing Agencies.
  • Every new client signs, then gets a kickoff package automatically, on the same SLA every time. See Client Onboarding Automation for Marketing Agencies.
  • Monthly client reports draft themselves from live ad-spend, analytics, and CRM data. You review and send. See Automated Client Reporting for Marketing Agencies.
  • Open proposals and quotes get followed up on a schedule, without anyone remembering. See Proposal and Quote Follow-Up for Marketing Agencies.

You own all of it. We document every prompt and connection so your team can read, change, and audit it.
sections.howWeInstall:
Three phases:

  1. Audit, free, about 10 minutes. Score your AI readiness across the Four Cs and surface the three highest-leverage automations for your agency. Run the audit.
  2. Install, 10 business days. We wire up your tools, capture your business and voice, and ship your first working automations. The free 60-Second Lead Engine is usually first because it pays for itself in one booked call.
  3. Operate, ongoing. We run it, watch it, fix it, and ship one new automation per week, measured against your kickoff baseline. See plans.
sections.expectedResults:
For a typical marketing agency in the 3 to 25 staff range, we plan against this baseline:

  • Recover 40 or more hours a month inside the team's calendar, or we keep working free until you do.
  • Cut lead response time from hours (often days) to under 60 seconds.
  • Reduce monthly report-building time by 70 to 90 percent.
  • Standardize onboarding so the worst client experience matches the best.

We do not promise revenue lift. Lift depends on close rate, offer, and market. We promise the recovered hours and the SLA.
sections.faq:
Who owns the system?

You do. Every prompt, connection, and automation lives in your accounts. If you ever stop working with us, you keep all of it.

What if something breaks?

We monitor every workflow and fix it. You get alerts and a clear log of every run, no silent failures.

How fast until ROI?

First automations are live within 10 business days. We measure recovered hours against your kickoff baseline over the following 30 days. The Lead Engine alone usually pays back within the first booked call.
